title: Enabling bi-directional writing for an RTL language
breadcrumbs: Enabling bi-directional writing for an RTL language
text: Enabling bi-directional writing for an RTL language Right-to-left (RTL) languages are written from right to left of the screen, such as Arabic, Hebrew, and Persian languages. English is a Left-to-right (LTR) language. Bi-directional writing supports both RTL and LTR languages simultaneously. By default, bi-directional writing is disabled in Uniface IDE editor. To enable bi-directional writing for an RTL language, you need to update the usys.ini file. Edit the usys.ini file. Under the [Widgets] > SourceEdit widget definition, change bidirectional=off to bidirectional=on as shown below: Explain this code ;usys.ini [widgets] ... SourceEdit=usrcedit(font=procboxfont;language=uniface;indenttabs=on;indentsize=4;indentguides=off;bidirectional=off;) Copy You can now write both from right-to-left and left-to-right directions.
